quick_facts.label: Position and cadre context
quick_facts.value: Assistant Commandant is a Group A, Gazetted, Non-Ministerial rank in CISF; a CISF Assistant Commandant/Fire vacancy notice identifies the corresponding revised pay level as Level 10 under the 7th CPC.

quick_facts.label: Primary external entry route
quick_facts.value: For the executive Assistant Commandant route, UPSC conducts the Central Armed Police Forces (ACs) Examination and allocates successful candidates across CAPFs, including CISF, subject to the notification, merit, medical fitness, vacancies and force preferences.

quick_facts.label: Education baseline
quick_facts.value: The UPSC CAPF (AC) 2025 notice required a bachelor's degree or an equivalent qualification; candidates awaiting qualifying results could be provisionally admitted subject to producing passing proof at the prescribed stage. Eligibility must be checked against the notification for the candidate's own examination year.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages
quick_facts.value: The UPSC CAPF (AC) process includes a written examination, Physical Standards/Physical Efficiency Tests, an Interview/Personality Test, and medical standards testing. Final merit is based on written-examination and interview/personality-test marks, while medical fitness remains mandatory for appointment.

quick_facts.label: 2026 examination timing
quick_facts.value: UPSC’s examination-notification archive lists CAPF (AC) Examination 2026 as notified on February 20, 2026, with the written examination scheduled for July 19, 2026; the application deadline listed there was March 12, 2026 at 18:00. These are cycle-specific dates, not a standing annual timetable.

sections.heading: What a CISF Assistant Commandant does
paragraphs: An Assistant Commandant is a supervisory operational officer. Under the CISF Rules, the officer assists the Commandant, may perform the Commandant’s functions when required, assists a Deputy Commandant where applicable, and is responsible for the efficiency, discipline and morale of personnel under the officer as well as security of the undertaking or duties entrusted to the officer.

CISF’s security charter gives the operational setting for that responsibility: access control, perimeter security, protection of designated critical installations, prevention and control of crime, escorting vital consignments, personnel protection and counter-terror quick-response duties. The exact portfolio varies by posting and unit.
bullets: Lead, deploy and maintain discipline among personnel under command.
Support unit security management and take delegated command responsibilities.
Apply security procedures at critical infrastructure and public-facing protected sites.

sections.heading: Entering through UPSC CAPF (AC)
paragraphs: The canonical external route is the UPSC Central Armed Police Forces (Assistant Commandants) Examination. In the 2025 notice, both men and women were eligible; the notice set the ordinary age band at 20–25 years on its specified cut-off date, with listed category and service relaxations. It also required Indian citizenship subject to stated exceptions for subjects of Nepal or Bhutan, and a bachelor’s degree or equivalent. Requirements can change, so applicants should rely on the notice for their own cycle.

The written stage has two papers: General Ability and Intelligence (250 marks) and General Studies, Essay and Comprehension (200 marks). Candidates who qualify the written examination proceed to PST/PET; the 2025 PET included timed 100-metre and 800-metre runs, long jump, and—only for men—shot put. Candidates qualifying the physical stages are called for the UPSC Interview/Personality Test, worth 150 marks, followed by medical standards testing.

There is also an internal path into the AC (Executive) grade: the Ministry of Home Affairs stated in 2022 that 50% of AC (Executive) posts were filled through promotion, including LDCE. That is a route for eligible serving CISF personnel and is distinct from the UPSC CAPF (AC) external-entry route.
bullets: Track UPSC’s notification and apply through the official UPSC application portal specified for that cycle.
Prepare simultaneously for the written papers, physical standards/efficiency requirements, interview and medical fitness.
Treat age limits, physical standards, vacancies, preferences, fees and deadlines as notification-specific.

sections.heading: Skills that match the role and examination
paragraphs: The role calls for command judgement, personnel leadership, discipline, security awareness and the ability to operate within formal procedures, because the CISF Rules assign Assistant Commandants responsibility for personnel efficiency, morale and undertaking security. The selection syllabus also tests logical reasoning, quantitative aptitude, data interpretation, general science, current affairs, essay writing and English-language comprehension—useful indicators of the academic and communication preparation expected of applicants.

Physical readiness is not merely desirable: candidates must clear prescribed PST/PET and medical standards before appointment. Applicants should therefore build structured endurance, speed, basic power and injury-conscious conditioning alongside written-exam preparation.
bullets: Team leadership, calm decision-making and accountability.
Security assessment, access-control awareness and incident response.
Clear written communication for essays, reports and operational coordination.
Quantitative reasoning, general awareness and disciplined physical preparation.

sections.heading: Posting environment
paragraphs: CISF protects a broad range of critical infrastructure rather than operating in a single office environment. Its official description identifies deployments at nuclear and space establishments, airports, seaports, power plants, government buildings, heritage sites, the Delhi Metro, prisons and Parliament-related security duties. Consequently, an Assistant Commandant may work in field units, transport hubs, industrial or strategic installations, headquarters-linked assignments or training environments, depending on organisational need.

This is a nationally deployable uniformed service. CISF states that it is present across almost all states and runs reserve battalions and training institutions; applicants should therefore expect location, shift pattern, operational exposure and unit duties to vary materially by posting.
bullets: Potentially public-facing work at airports, metro systems and protected public sites.
Potentially high-security work at strategic, industrial, energy and infrastructure installations.
Transferable postings and operational command responsibilities rather than a fixed-location career.

sections.heading: Career progression context
paragraphs: Assistant Commandant is an officer rank within the CISF executive structure. Official CISF promotion orders document promotion from Assistant Commandant (Executive) to Deputy Commandant (Executive), demonstrating a higher officer grade beyond AC. Actual advancement depends on applicable recruitment/promotion rules, seniority, eligibility, performance, vacancies, prescribed training and other service conditions; it should not be treated as automatic.

For context on entry into the AC grade from within the force, the Ministry of Home Affairs stated that half of AC (Executive) posts were filled by promotion, including through LDCE, and that eligibility for promotion from Inspector included regular service, medical category, prescribed training and duty-battalion service. Those historical details describe the internal feeder route, not guaranteed promotion conditions for every future cycle.
bullets: A documented next officer rank is Deputy Commandant (Executive).
Promotional prospects are governed by service rules and organisational vacancies, not only time served.
Serving personnel may access AC (Executive) through promotion/LDCE under the applicable rules.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: A CISF notice for Assistant Commandant/Fire describes the post as revised Pay Level 10, with the 7th CPC matrix range of ₹56,100–₹1,77,500. This is pay-matrix context rather than a promise of take-home pay: gross and net pay vary with admissible allowances, duty location, accommodation, deductions, service status and future government revisions. The cited notice concerned AC/Fire on deputation, but it is official evidence for the Group A Assistant Commandant Level 10 pay context.

Candidates should verify the appointment offer, current pay orders and applicable CISF/MHA instructions for the precise compensation package at joining. Do not infer a fixed monthly in-hand salary solely from the Level 10 range.
bullets: Official cited pay context: revised Pay Level 10, ₹56,100–₹1,77,500.
Allowances and deductions are variable and can change with policy and posting.
Confirm the applicable appointment terms and current government orders before making financial decisions.
